name: Cod Chowder
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the fish with cold water and pat dry with paper towels.
2. Cut the fish into 1/2-inch cubes and set aside.
3. In a small skillet, sauté the bacon until crisp. Remove the bacon from the skillet and blot with paper towels, then crumble it. Set aside.
4. Discard all but 2 tablespoons of bacon drippings in the skillet.
5. Add onion to the skillet and sauté until tender but not brown. Remove from heat.
6. In a deep saucepan, combine onion and potatoes. Add water; cover and bring to a gentle boil.
7. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes. Add fish and potatoes; cover and bring to boil again.
8. Reduce heat and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until fish and potatoes are done.
9. In a small bowl, combine flour and clam juice; stir with a fork until smooth.
10. Add the flour mixture to the fish and potato mixture, stirring constantly to blend.
11. Stir in evaporated milk, butter, salt, and white pepper. Continue heating over medium heat until chowder thickens.
12. Garnish with crumbled bacon before serving. Makes 6 servings.
